seed_instruction: Create an email to a professor introducing yourself and your goals.
org_constraint: The email must consist of exactly three paragraphs.
new_constraint: The email should consist of 10 sentences, with each paragraph containing at least four sentences.
instruction: Create an email to a professor introducing yourself and your goals. The email must consist of exactly three paragraphs. The email should consist of 10 sentences, with each paragraph containing at least four sentences.
response:
Subject: Introduction and Research Interests from [Your Name]

Dear Professor [Last Name], I hope this message finds you well and that your semester is off to a productive start. My name is [Your Name], and I am a junior majoring in [Your Major] at [Your University]. I have been following your recent work on [Specific Topic] with great interest for quite some time now. It is truly inspiring to see how your research is shaping the future of our field.

I am writing to express my strong desire to join your lab as a research assistant this upcoming fall semester. My primary goal is to gain hands-on experience in [Specific Skill or Method] while contributing meaningfully to your ongoing projects. I believe that my background in [Your Relevant Course or Project] has prepared me well for the challenges of your laboratory. Furthermore, I am eager to learn from your mentorship and develop a deeper understanding of [Specific Area of Study].

Thank you very much for considering my request and for the time you take to read this introduction. I have attached my resume and transcript for your review and would be happy to provide any additional materials you might need. I would welcome the opportunity to discuss how my skills and goals align with your current research needs. Please let me know if you have any availability for a brief meeting next week. I look forward to the possibility of working together and contributing to your esteemed team.
